children.text: Linear Regression is the bedrock of Machine Learning. At its heart, it's about finding the
Imagine you're trying to predict house prices. You have the size of the house (X) and the price (Y). The goal of Linear Regression is to find the best possible line that describes this data:
. We calculate the 'Residuals' (the distance between our line and the actual data points) and minimize the sum of their squares.

questions.question: If the residuals are very large, what does that say about our model?
options: The model is a perfect fit
The model is doing a poor job of capturing the relationship

questions.question: Given the model $y = 5x + 2$, what is the predicted $y$ when $x = 4$?
Which parameter in $y = mx + c$ represents the 'bias' or 'intercept'?
questions.explanation: The 'c' (or beta-0) is the intercept, representing the value of y when x is zero.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the OLS method?
options: Minimize the sum of squared residuals
Find the average of all data points
questions.explanation: Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) minimizes the sum of squared vertical distances between the observed data and the fitted line.

apply.instruction: Your challenge: Implement a simple linear regression calculation manually before moving to automated tools.

milestones.title: Calculate Mean
milestones.description: Calculate the mean of X and Y values from the provided sample dataset.

milestones.title: Solve for Slope
milestones.description: Use the formula for 'm' using the calculated means.
